The '''''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' soundtrack''' was recorded at the Abbey Road studios in January and February 1983 by the [[London Symphony Orchestra]]. The score won another Oscar nomination for [[John Williams]]. [[Herbert W. Spencer]], [[Wikipedia:Thomas Newman|Thomas Newman]]<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.npr.org/2015/10/17/449417429/composer-thomas-newman-teams-with-spielberg-for-bridge-of-spies|text=When John Williams Can't Go, Whom Does Spielberg Call? Thomas Newman|date=2015-10-17|work=NPR.org|archivedate=20230102014205}}</ref> and [[Wikipedia:Gordon Langford|Gordon Langford]] served as orchestrators. Engineer [[Eric Tomlinson]], music editor [[Kenneth Wannberg]], and record supervisor [[Lionel Newman]] again reprised their respective duties. Because of the decrease of popularity for the vinyl format, ''Return of the Jedi'', which has the longest score of the [[original trilogy]], was only released on one LP instead of two like the scores for its predecessors, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

|Jabba source. Called "Lapti Nek" on OST and Anthology.
Multiple versions exist:

*The version used in the original movie, sung by Annie Arbogast is unreleased but can be heard in ''From Star Wars to Jedi''.
*Instrumental of film version, unreleased but can be heard on the ''Return of the Jedi'' radio drama
*The English version "Fancy Man" is unreleased but can be heard in two documentaries (''From Star Wars to Jedi'' and ''Classic Creatures'')
*The album version is a complete rerecording, this time sung by Michele Gruska

==Star Wars Trilogy: The Original Soundtrack Anthology==
{{Main|Star Wars Trilogy: The Original Soundtrack Anthology}}
In 1993, Twentieth Century Fox Film Scores released a four-CD box set containing music from the original ''Star Wars'' trilogy. Disc three in the set was devoted to ''Return of the Jedi'', with further tracks on disc four.

==Track listing for the Special Edition release==
The two-CD expanded edition on RCA (1997) and [[Sony]] ([[September 21]], [[2004]]) contained the 1997 remix of the score.<ref name="SWArchive-Sony Classical Presents Star Wars Trilogy Soundtracks on CD">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/misc/news20040820.html|text=Sony Classical Presents ''Star Wars '' Trilogy Soundtracks on CD}}</ref> (It was falsely advertised with the legend "Now available on RCA (1997) and Sony Classical (2004) presenting the complete score.")

====Disc one====
#20th Century Fox Fanfare (Alfred Newman, 1954) – 0:22
#Main Title/Approaching the Death Star/Tatooine Rendezvous* – 9:21
#*contains the hidden track "Death Star in Disarray" (source music counterpart to "Darth Vader's Death") at 5:04–5:38
#The Droids Are Captured*1:21

==Track Listing for The Remastered Edition==

A remastered version of the soundtrack was released by [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Records|Walt Disney Records]] on May 4, 2018.<ref name=":1">{{WebCite|publication-place=Burbank, California|url=https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/disney-music-group-set-to-release-first-6-remastered-star-wars-original-motion-picture-soundtracks-on-may-4-300617275.html|text=Disney Music Group Set To Release First 6 Remastered Star Wars Original Motion Picture Soundtracks On May 4|date=2018-03-21|archivedate=20230102013031|agency=PR Newswire}}</ref> supervised by [[Wikipedia: Shawn Murphy (sound engineer)|Shawn Murphy]] and [[Skywalker Sound]], this version was transferred to a new hi-resolution (24/192).<ref name="SW-Remasters of the First 6 Star Wars Soundtracks Coming May 4">{{SW|url=news/remasters-of-the-first-6-star-wars-soundtracks-coming-may-4|text=Remasters of the First 6 ''Star Wars'' Soundtracks Coming May 4}}</ref> This remaster was newly assembled from the highest-quality tapes available, rather than sourced from the existing 1983 album masters.<ref name="disney marketing video on remasters">{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@DisneyMusicVEVO|name=Disney Music VEVO|video=WSOzvCtaHmg|text=John Williams - Star Wars: Remastering The Score}}</ref>

#Main Title (The Main Story Continues) - 5:09
#Into The Trap - 2:36
#Luke And Leia - 4:44
#Parade Of The Ewoks - 3:25
#Han Solo Returns (At The Court Of Jabba The Hutt) - 4:10
#Lapti Nek (By Jabba's Palace Band) - 2:49
#The Forest Battle - 4:01
#Rebel Briefing - 2:22
#The Emperor - 2:41
#The Return Of The Jedi - 5:02
#Ewok Celebration And Finale - 8:00

Total Time: 44:59
